questions.stem: Synonym of INNOCUOUS:

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: monitoring / technology / improved / has / safety / mine
options: Improved technology has mine safety monitoring
Monitoring mine safety has technology improved
Technology has improved mine safety monitoring
Mine safety monitoring technology has improved
questions.solution: Technology has improved mine safety monitoring

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Synonym of BELLIGERENT:

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: data / protect / essential / is / privacy / to
options: Essential is to protect data privacy
Data privacy is essential to protect

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Synonym of PRUDENT:

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: education / the / to / key / is / success
options: Education is the key to success
To success the key is education
Success is the key to education
questions.solution: Education is the key to success

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Synonym of PRAGMATIC:

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: mining / India / in / industry / is / a / important / sector
options: An important sector in India is mining industry
Mining industry is a important sector in India
Mining is an important sector in India
In India mining is important sector
questions.solution: Mining is an important sector in India

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Synonym of ALLEVIATE:

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: team / successfully / the / completed / project / the
options: Successfully the team completed the project
The team successfully completed the project
The project successfully completed the team
Completed the project the team successfully
questions.solution: The team successfully completed the project

questions.solution: Costs should reduce without compromising quality

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: She is smarter than any student in the class.
options: She is the smartest than any student in the class.
She is smarter than any other student in the class.
She is smarter than any student in the class.
She is smarter than all student in the class.
questions.solution: She is smarter than any other student in the class.

questions.solution: A fully skewed BST behaves like a linked list, giving O(n) search.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The OSI model has how many layers?
questions.solution: OSI: Physical, Data Link, Network, Transport, Session, Presentation, Application.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Single Responsibility Principle (SRP):
options: A class should have only one reason to change
questions.solution: SRP: one class = one responsibility (high cohesion). Each change affects at most one class.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Operations on an empty stack: push 7, push 9, push 14, pop, push 21. The element now on top is:
questions.solution: After push 14 then pop (removes 14), pushing 21 leaves 21 on top (LIFO).

options: Detecting collision, sending jam signal, backing off (exponential backoff)
questions.solution: CSMA/CD: Carrier Sense Multiple Access / Collision Detection. Binary exponential backoff after collision.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Liskov Substitution Principle (LSP):
options: Derived classes must be substitutable for their base classes
questions.solution: LSP: child class should not weaken the base class contract (preconditions, postconditions).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A splay tree is:
options: A self-adjusting BST that moves accessed elements to the root
questions.solution: Splay tree: recently accessed elements near root (amortized O(log n)).

options: Reuse the same TCP connection for multiple requests
questions.solution: HTTP/1.1: keep-alive by default (reduces TCP handshake overhead).

options: The degree of interdependence between modules
questions.solution: Coupling (best→worst): data, stamp, control, external, common, content. Low coupling = good design.

questions.solution: Ukkonen's algorithm: O(n) for suffix tree construction.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Nagle's algorithm:
options: Bundles small outgoing TCP segments to reduce overhead
questions.solution: Nagle: delay sending if there's unACKed data and data < MSS (improves efficiency).

questions.stem: Operations on an empty stack: push 5, push 11, push 14, pop, push 25. The element now on top is:
questions.solution: After push 14 then pop (removes 14), pushing 25 leaves 25 on top (LIFO).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The maximum data rate of USB 3.0 is:
questions.solution: USB 2.0 = 480 Mbps. USB 3.0 = 5 Gbps. USB 3.1 Gen 2 = 10 Gbps. USB 3.2 = 20 Gbps.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Agile methodology emphasizes:
options: Iterative development, customer collaboration, and responding to change
questions.solution: Agile Manifesto: individuals & interactions, working software, customer collaboration, responding to change.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A hash table with 100 slots holds 60 keys. Its load factor is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: DSCP (Differentiated Services Code Point):
options: 6-bit field in IP header for QoS classification (64 classes)
questions.solution: DSCP: replaces ToS byte. EF (Expedited Forwarding) for low-loss/low-latency, AF (Assured Forwarding) for data.

questions.stem: The decimal value of the hexadecimal number 64₁₆ is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: User stories follow the format:
options: As a [role], I want [feature] so that [benefit]
questions.solution: User story: role-want-benefit format. Acceptance criteria define done.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Operations on an empty stack: push 3, push 11, push 14, pop, push 25. The element now on top is:
questions.solution: After push 14 then pop (removes 14), pushing 25 leaves 25 on top (LIFO).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The memory address register (MAR) holds:
options: The address of the memory location to access
questions.solution: MAR: holds address for memory read/write. Connected to address bus.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The characteristic equation of the D flip-flop is:
questions.solution: D FF: output follows input on clock edge. Q(t+1) = D.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Binary search on a sorted array of n elements runs in:
questions.solution: Each comparison halves the search space, giving logarithmic time — but the array must be sorted.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A Turing machine has:
options: An infinite tape and a read/write head
questions.solution: Turing machine = infinite tape + R/W head + finite control.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A cache miss occurs when:
options: Requested data is not found in the cache
questions.solution: Cache miss = data must be fetched from next level (L2/L3/RAM).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Multilevel feedback queue allows:
options: Processes to move between queues (prevents starvation)
questions.solution: Feedback: aging mechanism. Process that waits too long moves to higher-priority queue.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The worst-case time of finding the kth smallest element in an unsorted array is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Memory access = 200 ns, page-fault rate = 0.002, fault service = 10 ms. The effective access time is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The worst-case time complexity of mergesort for n=64 is:
questions.solution: Mergesort always O(n log n). For n=64: O(64 log 64).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A link of 100 Mbps has a round-trip time of 20 ms. The bandwidth-delay product is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A multicore processor has:
options: Multiple CPU cores on a single chip
questions.solution: Multicore = multiple cores per socket (e.g., quad-core, octa-core).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The JOIN operation combines:
options: Rows from two or more tables based on a related column
questions.solution: JOIN = combine tables on matching condition (INNER, LEFT, RIGHT, FULL).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Slow start doubles the congestion window:
questions.solution: Slow start: cwnd ← cwnd × 2 per RTT (or +1 per ACK). Until ssthresh.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The difference between HTTP and HTTPS is:
options: HTTPS uses a different port only

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The default gateway is:
options: The router that connects a local network to other networks
questions.solution: Default gateway = exit router for traffic not destined to local network.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Jumbo frames have MTU:
options: Up to 9000 bytes (improves throughput for large transfers)
questions.solution: Jumbo frames: reduces CPU overhead (fewer interrupts, less header overhead). Requires end-to-end support.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: DNS resolution starts with:
options: The resolver querying the local cache first
questions.solution: DNS: cache → stub resolver → recursive resolver → root → TLD → authoritative.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: TCP is a:
questions.solution: TCP = connection-oriented, reliable (ACKs, retransmission). UDP = connectionless.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The UDP header has:
options: 8 bytes (source port, dest port, length, checksum)
questions.solution: UDP header: src(16) + dst(16) + len(16) + cksum(16) = 64 bits = 8 bytes.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The memory management unit (MMU) translates:
questions.solution: MMU: CPU generates virtual addresses, MMU translates to physical.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A deque supports:
options: Insertion and deletion at both ends (double-ended queue)
questions.solution: Deque: push_front, push_back, pop_front, pop_back. Can be implemented with circular array or doubly linked list.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Spanning Tree Protocol (STP) prevents:
options: Broadcast storms and loops in Ethernet networks (redundant links)
questions.solution: STP: IEEE 802.1D. Elects root bridge, blocks redundant ports. RSTP (802.1w) = faster convergence.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The system call interface:
options: Provides user programs access to kernel services
questions.solution: System calls: API between user space and kernel (e.g., read/write/fork/exec).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The stack ADT supports:
options: Push (insert top) and pop (remove top) - LIFO
Insert and delete at both ends

questions.stem: A 200×20 array of 4-byte elements occupies how much memory?

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The decimal equivalent of binary 10110 is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In the graph shown (edges A–B, A–C, B–D, C–D), a breadth-first search from A, visiting neighbours alphabetically, gives the order:
questions.solution: BFS from A visits A, then its neighbours B and C, then D: A, B, C, D.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Quicksort attains its O(n²) worst case when:
options: The pivot is consistently the smallest or largest element
The array is large but random
The pivot is always the median
questions.solution: Maximally unbalanced partitions (e.g. sorted input with first/last pivot) give O(n²).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: DNS is responsible for:
options: Translating domain names to IP addresses
questions.solution: The Domain Name System resolves human-readable names to IP addresses.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Race-around condition occurs in:
options: JK flip-flop (when J=K=1 and clock pulse too long)
questions.solution: Race-around: JK with J=K=1 oscillates if clock pulse > propagation delay. Solved by master-slave.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Two-phase locking (2PL) protocol:
options: Locks and releases at any time
Expanding phase (acquire locks) → Shrinking phase (release locks, no new acquires)
questions.solution: 2PL: guarantees serializability. Strict 2PL: all exclusive locks held until commit (prevents cascading aborts).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The class P consists of problems:
options: Solvable in polynomial time by a deterministic Turing machine
questions.solution: P = DTIME(n^k) for some k (polynomial time on DTM).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The decimal value of the hexadecimal number F₁₆ is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Halting problem for Turing machines is:
options: Undecidable (no algorithm can solve it for all inputs)
questions.solution: Halting problem (Turing, 1936): undecidable. No program can determine if another program halts.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: SIMD extensions include:
questions.solution: SIMD: single instruction operates on multiple data elements (packed in wide registers).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Z-algorithm (Z-function):
options: Finds all occurrences of a pattern in O(n+m) by computing longest common prefix between string and its suffixes
questions.solution: Z[i] = longest common prefix of S and S[i..]. Used for pattern matching (concat pattern + $ + text).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Factory Method pattern:
options: Defines an interface for creating objects, letting subclasses decide which class to instantiate
questions.solution: Factory Method: encapsulates object creation (virtual constructor).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Burndown chart shows:
options: Remaining work (y-axis) over time (x-axis) in a sprint or project
questions.solution: Burndown: ideal line (straight from start to zero) vs actual line (remaining effort each day).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The RARP protocol:
options: Resolves MAC addresses to IP addresses (reverse of ARP)
questions.solution: RARP: used by diskless workstations at boot time (obsolete, replaced by DHCP/BOOTP).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Error recovery in parsing:
options: Attempts to continue parsing after a syntax error by skipping tokens
questions.solution: Error recovery: panic mode (skip until sync token), phrase-level, error productions.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The 'grep' command is used for:
questions.solution: grep = global regex print (pattern matching in files).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Quick sort worst-case occurs when:
options: The pivot is always the minimum or maximum element
questions.solution: Worst case: pivot always min or max = O(n²). Sorted array with first element as pivot = O(n²).
